activeGoal: Add end-to-end observability across the sidepanel SSE/transcript pipeline to diagnose missing, duplicate, replayed, or o
designSummary: ArchiMate 3.1 design for ELM-002 adds a cross-stage Transcript Pipeline Observability Service over the existing extension brownfield seams. The design extends existing shared transport/normalization, sidepanel ingestion/acceptance, transcript projection, diagnostics export, and render analysis modules rather than introducing a new standalone subsystem. New software units are modeled for shared stream contract tracing, sidepanel stream acceptance tracing, transcript projection tracing, diagnostics export assembly, and final render analysis. The design preserves the current sidepanel UX contract by keeping observability evidence passive to rendering semantics and exporting correlated time- and content-oriented trace evidence that allows engineers to identify the earliest stage where transcript content becomes missing, duplicated, replayed, or out of order.
designDecisions: ADR-1 Brownfield fit decision: extend existing modules extension/src/shared/api.ts, extension/src/shared/protocol.ts, extension/src/sidepanel/useSidepanelController.ts, extension/src/sidepanel/model.ts, extension/src/sidepanel/reasoningTimeline.ts, extension/src/sidepanel/diagnostics.ts, extension/src/sidepanel/reasoningTimelineView.tsx, and extension/src/sidepanel/components/stage/MainStage.tsx. Rejected creating a new standalone observability subsystem because the required ownership seams already exist and a new subsystem would increase coupling and UX regression risk.

ADR-2 Observability contract decision: introduce a Transcript Pipeline Observability Service and Transcript Trace Evidence data object so the same logical transcript-bearing item can be correlated across transport receipt, event generation, normalization, sidepanel acceptance, transcript projection, diagnostics export, and final render analysis.

ADR-3 UX preservation decision: diagnostics remain passive to the user experience. Instrumentation may add trace metadata, console/export evidence, and render-path analysis, but must not change normal sidepanel transcript content, transcript ordering semantics, or visible interaction behavior.

audit.summary: Audit passed for commit 5c0b3f8 (ELM-002 rework batch covering TASK-024, TASK-025, TASK-026, TASK-027, TASK-028). The intent baseline is sufficient and complete across core layers. Implementation reality exposes explicit ArchitectureID evidence for ELM-FUNC-EXT-CAPTURE-TRANSPORT-CANONICAL-TRACE in extension/src/shared/api.ts, ELM-FUNC-SP-TRACE-STREAM-ACCEPTANCE-FRONTIER and ELM-FUNC-SP-ASSEMBLE-CORRELATED-TRANSCRIPT-DIAGNOSTICS in extension/src/sidepanel/useSidepanelController.ts, ELM-FUNC-SP-TRACE-INCREMENTAL-TRANSCRIPT-PROJECTION in extension/src/sidepanel/reasoningTimeline.ts, and ELM-FUNC-SP-ANALYZE-FINAL-TRANSCRIPT-RENDER in extension/src/sidepanel/reasoningTimelineView.tsx and MainStage.tsx. The strict tagged intent-verification gate now passes because verifiedIntentIds includes Requirement ELM-002 and all required architecture IDs. Sidepanel automated tests also preserve passive observability and visible UX semantics through transcript continuity, debounced history behavior, chat-first shell cues, and unified transcript stream rendering checks.
